summary: Crystal‑clear audience tape of an epic Halloween show – stellar second set highlighted by a monster St. Stephen and a smoking Help>Slip>Kenn.
review: The recording is an exceptionally clean AUD capture that lets the band’s festive Halloween vibe shine, especially during the drummers’ space jam that segues into a thunderous St. Stephen.  The performance is consistently high‑energy with standout moments like Help>Slip>Kenn, Brown Eyed Women and a rousing Not Fade Away, while the first set receives modest praise only.  Overall the tape delivers both superb sound quality and an unforgettable live experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars, praise both musical performance and audio fidelity, and only a single low‑rating comment mentions minor setlist slip; this consensus yields a strong positive rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners repeatedly note the tape is clearer and more balanced than many SBDs, with crisp drums, chime‑like keys, good instrument separation and no noticeable drop‑outs.

poor_songs: Help > Slip > Franklin's Tower (rushed in one review)
show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight Halloween set that builds from a lively first half into an unforgettable drummers‑space jam, culminating in the last-ever live St. Stephen; the second set maintains momentum with classic pairings like Help>Slip>Kenn and a fiery Not Fade Away.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his powerful guitar tone, soaring solos on St. Stephen and the Help>Slip>Kenn sequence, and for “ripping” in ’83‑84.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted as being “on top of his game,” driving the space jam and delivering tight bass lines throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Described as chime‑like with bright piano/organ tones that add sparkle, especially during the second set jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Airto’s guest percussion plus Mickey & Bill’s wild “weird drums” created a legendary space segment; Mickey’s tapping on Estimated Prophet noted.
